title: Score previously-produced clustering solutions against the original features
summary: Loads the input CSV and the memberships.csv from a prior segment run, then computes per-solution cluster statistics (size, within-cluster dissimilarity, silhouette, pearson Îł) and writes a summary CSV.
description:
`evaluate` is the companion of `segment`. It re-reads the original input CSV, loads `memberships.csv` from the matching `run_dir` / `run_name`, computes pairwise dissimilarities under the chosen `metric`, and scores each solution named in `solutions`.

Use this to compare solutions across k or across methods after a `segment` call.
usage:
[["clustering::evaluate"]]
input = "<csv-path>"
vars = ["<feature1>", ...]
solutions = ["<run_name>_<method>_k<k>", ...]
run_dir = "<output-root>"
run_name = "<run-id>"
metric = "euclidean"

arguments.name: input
arguments.description: Path to the same CSV that was passed to segment.

arguments.name: vars
arguments.description: Feature column names used to build the dissimilarity matrix.

arguments.name: solutions
arguments.description: Solution column names to score; must exist in `memberships.csv`.

arguments.name: run_dir
arguments.description: Root directory of the prior segment run.

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Run name used by the prior segment run.

arguments.name: headers
arguments.description: Whether the input CSV has a header row.

details:
## Output scores
For each named solution, `evaluate` reports:
- `n` / `n_clusters` â€” retained-row count and cluster count (excluding any noise label).
- `avg_within` / `avg_between` â€” average within- vs between-cluster dissimilarity.
- `silhouette` â€” average silhouette width.
- `pearson_gamma` â€” Pearson's Îł between pairwise distance and same-cluster indicator. Stronger separation â†’ closer to 1.

## Metric
- `euclidean` (default) and `manhattan` operate directly on the feature matrix.
- `gower` handles mixed numeric / categorical columns via per-column normalisation.
value: Writes `<run_dir>/ocinfo/<run_name>_clustering/evaluate.csv` with one row per solution and the score columns listed above. Emits `event.run.started`, optional `event.warning` per-solution errors, and `event.run.finished`.
notes: Solution names must match the ones produced by the preceding segment run exactly â€” they are `<run_name>_<method>_k<k>`.
A per-solution error (e.g. unknown name) is captured as a warning; other solutions continue to be scored.

see_also.description: Produces the memberships.csv that evaluate scores.

examples.title: Evaluate two k-means solutions
examples.body:
[["clustering::evaluate"]]
input = "./data/respondents.csv"
vars = ["q1", "q2", "q3", "q4"]
solutions = ["kmeans_demo_kmeans_k3", "kmeans_demo_kmeans_k4"]
run_dir = "./runs"
run_name = "kmeans_demo"
metric = "euclidean"

fields.name: run_dir
fields.description: Root directory of the prior run.

fields.name: headers
fields.description: Whether the CSV has a header row.
